genre_refs: The Patriot (2000 film, Mel Gibson); American Revolution / colonial militia imagery
description: Movie-still graphic posted by the U.S. Department of Labor. The image is a sepia and teal color-graded film still from the 2000 movie 'The Patriot' showing Mel Gibson's character kneeling in a hay field, gripping the staff of a tattered early American flag (a 13-star flag with red-and-white stripes and a circle of white stars) that billows to the right. Smoke and a blurred field fill the background. Across the bottom in large distressed white serif capital letters with a shadow is the text 'HOLD THE LINE.' The tweet caption reads: 'We'll never stop fighting for the American Way of Life. AMERICA FIRST. AMERICANS FIRST.'
notable_text: HOLD THE LINE. / (caption) We’ll never stop fighting for the American Way of Life. AMERICA FIRST. AMERICANS FIRST.
